name: Angel Food Pudding Cake
recipeIngredient: 4 large egg whites, at room temperature
1 package chocolate pudding mix (low-fat or sugar-free)
1 cup whipped topping (low-fat or light, or homemade with heavy whipping cream and sweetener)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease a 10-inch angel food cake pan.
2. Pour the batter into the prepared pan and bake until set and golden brown, about 30-35 minutes.
3. Allow the cake to cool completely in the pan on a wire rack.
4. Remove the top of the cake, leaving about 1 inch (2.5 cm) at the top edge.
5. Cut off one inch from each side of the cake, being careful not to cut through the bottom layer.
6. Scoop out the center filling using an offset spatula or spoon.
7. In a cold metal or glass bowl, mix the chocolate pudding according to package instructions, adding milk as needed.
8. Stir in 1 cup (250 ml) of whipped topping until combined.
9. Fold in the scooped-out cake pieces.
10. Place the filling into the prepared shell and spread evenly.
11. Replace the top of the cake and trim the edges with additional whipped cream if desired.
12. Refrigerate for at least 2 hours before serving to allow the flavors to meld and the cake to set.
13. Serve chilled, garnished with extra whipped topping or fresh fruit if desired.
Note: This recipe is a variation on traditional angel food pudding cake, using leftover cake pieces as the filling. The amount of filling used can be adjusted based on personal preference.
